content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.9 `MemoryRun`
Document lines: 501-539

### 3.9 `MemoryRun`

Audit trail и debug слой.

Нужен, чтобы видеть:
- какой prompt сработал
- на какой модели
- что именно было retrieved
- какой budget был выделен
- какие ops были предложены
- какие ops реально применились
- где pipeline сломался

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `run_id`
- `namespace_id`
- `run_type: route | retrieve_update | canonicalize | construct_notes | reconcile_notes | extract_claims | reconcile_claims | update_profile | update_tasks | consolidate_notes | repair_memory | apply_retention | read_plan | compose_answer | compact`
- `trigger_mode: hot_path | background | manual | slow_path`
- `source_ids_json`
- `retrieved_item_ids_json`
- `retrieval_budget_json`
- `prompt_name`
- `prompt_version`
- `model_name`
- `input_hash`
- `output_json`
- `applied_ops_json`
- `repair_actions_json`
- `latency_ms`
- `token_input`
- `token_output`
- `status: success | failed | partial`
- `error_text`
- `created_at`

Инварианты:
- run log не должен исчезать silently
- `input_hash` полезен для идемпотентности и retry safety

        [0] type=DOC_DIGEST title=MemoryRun provides audit trail and debugging for memory pipeline runs summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `MemoryRun` is the audit trail and debug layer for memory pipeline executions, recording which prompt/model ran, what was retrieved, allocated budget, proposed and applied ops, and where the pipeline failed. Recommended fields include identifiers, run type, trigger mode, source/retrieved IDs, budget, prompt/model metadata, `input_hash`, outputs, applied/...[truncated 69 chars] scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.9 `MemoryRun`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining the role and recommended fields o...[truncated 14 chars] entities=entity:<0> keywords=MemoryRun,audit trail,debug layer,run_type,trigger_mode,input_hash,applied_ops_json,repair_actions_json tags=doc_digest,data_model,memoryrun,audit evidence=Audit trail и debug слой.
[1] type=DOC_DIGEST title=MemoryRun logs must persist and use input_hash for safe retries summary=`MemoryRun` invariants require that run logs not silently disappear, and note that `input_hash` is useful for idempotency and retry safety. scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.9 `MemoryRun`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, covering `MemoryRun` invariants. entities=entity:<0> keywords=MemoryRun,run log,input_hash,idempotency,retry safety tags=doc_digest,data_model,invariants,memoryrun evidence=`input_hash` полезен для идемпотентности и retry safety

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Source is immutable evidence layer for re-check and explainability summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Source` is the raw knowledge source for chat turns, tool outputs, imported notes, and external records; it serves as the primary evidence layer for re-check and explainability. Its design invariants emphasize no silent mutation, separate `observed_at` versus ingestion time, `content_hash` for dedupe, and an explicit delete policy. scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.1 `Source`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining the role and invariants of `Source`. keywords=Source,evidence layer,re-check,explainability,content_hash,observed_at,delete policy tags=data_model,source,evidence,invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Note invariants require one self-contained semantic fragment summary=`Note` invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md` require each note to be one self-contained semantic fragment that adds value beyond duplicating the source; `candidate_claims_json` is only hints, and a note may remain long-lived without becoming a claim if it stays useful for retrieval. scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.4 `Note`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, describing Note invariants and lifecycle expect...[truncated 7 chars] keywords=Note invariants,semantic fragment,candidate_claims_json,retrieval,source added value tags=doc_digest,data_model,invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Note represents durable intermediate semantic context summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Note` is an intermediate semantic memory fragment for important retrieval-useful context that should not yet be hardened into final claims, such as design directions, decision rationale, evolving plans, hypotheses, lessons, doc digests, and contextual framing. scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.4 `Note`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining when to use `Note` and how it differs ...[truncated 41 chars] keywords=Note,semantic memory fragment,retrieval,design direction,decision rationale,hypotheses,doc digests tags=doc_digest,data_model,note useCount=0

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
